Project.NAME: n0wss
Project.keywords: wss, websocket, secure-websocket, proxy, socks5, internet-access, iroh, sendme, nat-traversal, backpressure, graceful-shutdown, fast-fail, metrics, adapter-contract, task-tracker, burst-detection
Project.annotation: n0wss is a secure proxy service based on the sendme/iroh ecosystem. The approved v1 architecture exposes a local SOCKS5 endpoint, normalizes requests into ProxyIntent work items, resolves either iroh or WSS transport behind minimal stream and adapter contracts, and treats fallback, shutdown, timing, accurate reply mapping, task-scoped cleanup, and burst observability as first-class design concerns.

M-CLI.NAME: CliApp
M-CLI.purpose: Bootstrap the process, choose runtime mode, and coordinate graceful shutdown phases.
M-CLI.depends: M-CONFIG, M-OBS, M-AUTH, M-TLS, M-WSS-GATEWAY, M-SOCKS5, M-SOCKS5-UDP-ASSOCIATE, M-PROXY-BRIDGE, M-SESSION, M-TRANSPORT-ADAPTER-CONTRACT, M-DATAGRAM-CONTRACT, M-TRANSPORT-STREAM, M-TRANSPORT-TASK-TRACKER, M-UDP-ORIGDST-CONTRACT, M-UDP-ORIGDST-RUNTIME

fn-run.PURPOSE: Parse startup arguments and bootstrap the selected mode.

fn-runUntilShutdown.PURPOSE: Launch the selected runtime surface and keep it alive until cancellation.

fn-runOrigDstLiveUntilCancelled.PURPOSE: Bind and run one governed live origdst helper listener until cancellation.

fn-coordinateShutdown.PURPOSE: Stop accepts, drain work, and release transports in order.

type-ApplicationRunResult.PURPOSE: Represent final startup or shutdown outcomes.

type-OrigDstLiveLaunchResult.PURPOSE: Represent bounded live-helper listener metadata for one packet window.

type-CliRuntimeError.PURPOSE: Report deterministic runtime-launch and listener-lifecycle failures after bootstrap.

type-StartupArtifacts.PURPOSE: Carry config, observability, auth policy, session timings, and optional TLS startup handles.

M-CLI-BIN.NAME: CliBinary
M-CLI-BIN.purpose: Expose a deployable n0wss executable that forwards process arguments into the governed CLI runtime surface and returns stable exit codes.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Forward argv into the library CLI runtime entrypoint and map bootstrap or runtime errors to a shell-visible exit code.

M-CONFIG.NAME: Config
M-CONFIG.purpose: Load runtime configuration, TLS paths, auth material, queue limits, session limits, timeout policy, retry behavior, and burst thresholds.

fn-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Load and validate CLI arguments and environment configuration.

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed runtime configuration for client and server modes.

type-ClientTlsConfig.PURPOSE: Carries optional client trust-anchor material and endpoint-identity override for outbound WSS.

type-OrigDstLiveConfig.PURPOSE: Carries the exact live helper listener, payload capacity, operator user, and preserved-baseline fields.

export-config.PURPOSE: Expose validated immutable configuration to the rest of the application.

M-OBS.NAME: Observability
M-OBS.purpose: Initialize tracing, stable logging fields, metrics, sliding-window burst detection, and peak-rate diagnostics.

fn-initObservability.PURPOSE: Install tracing, counters, gauges, histograms, and field propagation behavior.

fn-recordBurst.PURPOSE: Record queue rejection timestamps into a sliding-window burst detector.

fn-resetPeak.PURPOSE: Reset peak-rate gauges on the configured maintenance interval.

type-ProxyMetricsHandle.PURPOSE: Holds counters, gauges, and histograms for proxy observability.

type-BurstDetectorHandle.PURPOSE: Tracks rejection spikes with peak-rate metrics and rate-limited alert logging.

M-AUTH.NAME: AuthPolicy
M-AUTH.purpose: Apply connection authentication, token or ticket validation, and secret redaction policy.

fn-validateHandshake.PURPOSE: Authorize an incoming or outgoing tunnel handshake.

fn-redactSecret.PURPOSE: Normalize sensitive values before logs or errors are emitted.

M-TLS.NAME: TlsContext
M-TLS.purpose: Load and expose TLS server and client contexts used by the WSS boundary.

fn-fromConfig.PURPOSE: Load certificate and key material into a deterministic TLS context.

fn-fromClientConfig.PURPOSE: Load client-only trust material into a deterministic outbound TLS context.

fn-accept.PURPOSE: Wrap a server-side TCP stream in TLS.

fn-connect.PURPOSE: Wrap a client-side TCP stream in TLS with SNI.

type-TlsContextHandle.PURPOSE: Typed holder for TLS materials and runtime context.

M-WSS-GATEWAY.NAME: WssGateway
M-WSS-GATEWAY.purpose: Compose TCP, TLS, websocket upgrade, and auth validation into WSS-backed transport streams under a shared adapter contract.
M-WSS-GATEWAY.depends: M-TLS, M-AUTH, M-OBS, M-UDP-EGRESS-RELAY, M-DATAGRAM-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR, M-DATAGRAM-CONTRACT, M-TRANSPORT-ADAPTER-CONTRACT, M-TRANSPORT-STREAM, M-TRANSPORT-TASK-TRACKER

fn-runServer.PURPOSE: Start the WSS listener and wrap accepted sessions into transport streams.

fn-openStream.PURPOSE: Establish an outbound WSS-backed transport stream.

fn-taskTracker.PURPOSE: Expose adapter-scoped task tracking for tests and shutdown.

type-ResolvedStream.PURPOSE: Represents a transport-agnostic resolved stream plus transport kind metadata.

M-IROH-ADAPTER.NAME: IrohAdapter
M-IROH-ADAPTER.purpose: Integrate with iroh/sendme transport primitives for direct or relay-backed stream creation under a shared adapter contract.

fn-openStream.PURPOSE: Open a direct or relay-backed transport stream to the remote peer.

fn-taskTracker.PURPOSE: Expose adapter-scoped task tracking for tests and shutdown.

fn-release.PURPOSE: Release iroh transport resources for a finished session.

type-TransportKind.PURPOSE: Identify Wss, IrohDirect, or IrohRelay for metrics and diagnostics.

M-SESSION.NAME: SessionManager
M-SESSION.purpose: Coordinate session registry, transport selection, pure lifecycle transitions, and stable top-level effect routing without becoming a transport or storage implementation.

fn-registerSession.PURPOSE: Create and track a new proxy session through the registry.

fn-resolveStream.PURPOSE: Choose iroh first and fall back to WSS sequentially.

fn-handleEvent.PURPOSE: Interpret pure state effects and dispatch them.

fn-shutdown.PURPOSE: Drain registry and close sessions during shutdown.

type-SessionManagerConfig.PURPOSE: Carry idle and graceful shutdown timing into the thin orchestrator.

type-SessionManagerError.PURPOSE: Report deterministic session-not-found, session-limit, draining-session, and transport-resolution failures.

type-SessionEffect.PURPOSE: Route pure state-machine outputs to registry, timer, or metric targets.

type-SessionHandle.PURPOSE: Provide cloneable access to one registry-owned session record.

type-SessionRecord.PURPOSE: Store mutable state, idle timestamps, and new-stream admission flags per session.

type-SessionState.PURPOSE: Represent Active, Closing, and Closed states.

type-SessionRegistry.PURPOSE: Concurrent map and semaphore for session storage and capacity control.

type-TransportSelector.PURPOSE: Sequential transport selection strategy with timeout and fallback policy.

type-TransportSelectError.PURPOSE: Describe cancellation, safety-timeout, or combined iroh and WSS resolution failures.

type-EffectHandler.PURPOSE: Stable top-level dispatcher for typed SessionEffect values.

type-RegistryCommand.PURPOSE: Local registry command enum with bounded variant count enforced by module tests.

M-SESSION-EFFECT-HANDLER.NAME: SessionEffectHandler
M-SESSION-EFFECT-HANDLER.purpose: Route top-level SessionEffect values to exactly one target component without embedding business logic.

fn-apply.PURPOSE: Route one SessionEffect to its owning target.

fn-applyAll.PURPOSE: Preserve effect order across a batch.

M-SESSION-EFFECTS.NAME: SessionEffects
M-SESSION-EFFECTS.purpose: Represent top-level typed session effects and component-scoped command enums consumed by the session orchestrator.

type-SessionEffect.PURPOSE: Represent one top-level typed effect routed by target component.

type-RegistryCommand.PURPOSE: Represent registry-specific commands emitted by pure transitions.

type-TimerCommand.PURPOSE: Represent timer-specific commands emitted by pure transitions.

type-MetricEvent.PURPOSE: Represent observability events emitted by pure transitions.

M-SESSION-REGISTRY.NAME: SessionRegistry
M-SESSION-REGISTRY.purpose: Store session handles, enforce max-session capacity, and execute registry-targeted session effects.

fn-tryReserve.PURPOSE: Reserve one session slot without waiting.

fn-insert.PURPOSE: Register one session handle and consume the reservation.

fn-remove.PURPOSE: Deregister a session and release capacity deterministically.

fn-drainAll.PURPOSE: Clear all tracked sessions and release capacity deterministically.

M-SESSION-STATE.NAME: SessionStateMachine
M-SESSION-STATE.purpose: Provide the pure per-session state machine used by the higher session orchestration layers.

fn-transition.PURPOSE: Apply one pure transition and emit ordered typed effects.

type-SessionState.PURPOSE: Represent Active, Closing, and Closed lifecycle states.

type-SessionEvent.PURPOSE: Represent the pure transition input surface.

M-SESSION-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR.NAME: SessionTransportSelector
M-SESSION-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR.purpose: Choose iroh first and fall back to WSS sequentially under bounded timeouts and explicit cancellation.

fn-openStream.PURPOSE: Resolve one transport stream with explicit iroh then WSS fallback behavior.

type-TransportSelector.PURPOSE: Sequential iroh then WSS selection strategy.

type-TransportSelectError.PURPOSE: Describe cancellation, safety-timeout, or combined transport failures.

M-SOCKS5.NAME: Socks5Proxy
M-SOCKS5.purpose: Expose a local SOCKS5 ingress surface, normalize requests into ProxyIntent work items, and emit accurate client-visible reply codes on pre-pump failures.

fn-runListener.PURPOSE: Bind and accept local SOCKS5 connections.

fn-parseRequest.PURPOSE: Parse SOCKS5 negotiation and CONNECT requests into a normalized ProxyIntent.

fn-mapReply.PURPOSE: Map ProxyError variants to exact SOCKS5 reply codes or no-reply post-pump outcomes.

fn-sendReply.PURPOSE: Emit a success or failure SOCKS5 reply without implicitly closing successful client sockets.

type-ProxyIntent.PURPOSE: Represents a validated local proxy request ready for queueing and tunnel forwarding.

type-Socks5Reply.PURPOSE: Exact SOCKS5 reply-code enum used for deterministic client-visible failures.

type-ErrorCategory.PURPOSE: Infrastructure, policy, target, or post-reply classification used for exhaustive reply mapping.

M-PROXY-BRIDGE.NAME: ProxyBridge
M-PROXY-BRIDGE.purpose: Consume ProxyIntent items, ask SessionManager for a generic stream, and relay bytes until completion or drain.

fn-runWorker.PURPOSE: Consume bounded ProxyIntent queue items and process them.

fn-processIntent.PURPOSE: Bound one proxied request by registration, resolution, timeout, reply semantics, and lifecycle notification.

fn-pumpBidirectional.PURPOSE: Move bytes between local proxy sockets and resolved transport streams.

fn-drainAll.PURPOSE: Drain active proxy streams during shutdown.

type-ProxyResult.PURPOSE: Represents successful completion or failure of a proxied request.

M-TRANSPORT-ADAPTER-CONTRACT.NAME: TransportAdapterContract
M-TRANSPORT-ADAPTER-CONTRACT.purpose: Define the shared adapter contract and typed transport request used by WSS and iroh transport implementations.

type-TransportRequest.PURPOSE: Carry peer label plus outbound target host and port for adapter resolution.

type-TransportAdapter.PURPOSE: Expose the shared open_stream contract for transport implementations.

M-TRANSPORT-STREAM.NAME: TransportStreamContract
M-TRANSPORT-STREAM.purpose: Define the transport-agnostic stream contract and resolved stream metadata shared by adapters and ProxyBridge.

type-TransportStream.PURPOSE: Expose the minimal transport-agnostic stream trait used by adapter outputs.

type-ResolvedStream.PURPOSE: Carry one transport stream plus transport kind metadata.

type-TransportKind.PURPOSE: Discriminate Wss, IrohDirect, and IrohRelay for diagnostics.

M-TRANSPORT-TASK-TRACKER.NAME: TransportTaskTracker
M-TRANSPORT-TASK-TRACKER.purpose: Provide adapter-scoped task tracking for cleanup-sensitive tests and shutdown coordination.

type-AdapterTaskTracker.PURPOSE: Wrap task tracking with adapter-scoped diagnostics.

M-TRANSPORT-MOD.NAME: TransportModule
M-TRANSPORT-MOD.purpose: Aggregate the governed transport helper modules behind one stable Rust module surface without adding transport logic.

export-adapter_contract.PURPOSE: Expose the shared adapter contract submodule.

export-stream.PURPOSE: Expose the transport stream contract submodule.

export-task_tracker.PURPOSE: Expose the adapter task tracker submodule.

M-CRATE-ROOT.NAME: CrateRoot
M-CRATE-ROOT.purpose: Expose the governed crate-root export surface and keep top-level module registration synchronized with the implementation.

fn-reexportModules.PURPOSE: Expose the current governed modules through the crate root without adding runtime logic.

M-RELEASE-DOCS.NAME: ReleaseDocs
M-RELEASE-DOCS.purpose: Prepare GitHub-facing release documentation, quickstart, changelog, and operator notes that match the current implementation.

fn-writeReadme.PURPOSE: Describe project overview, quickstart, and test entry points.

fn-writeChangelog.PURPOSE: Capture the current release scope for GitHub release notes.

M-GITHUB-RELEASE.NAME: GithubReleaseSurface
M-GITHUB-RELEASE.purpose: Define the GitHub CI and release automation surface used to validate and publish the first release candidate.

fn-verifyCiWorkflow.PURPOSE: Keep GitHub CI aligned with local required checks.

fn-defineReleaseFlow.PURPOSE: Describe tag and release-note expectations for GitHub publication.

M-SMOKE-E2E.NAME: SmokeE2E
M-SMOKE-E2E.purpose: Add a narrow smoke verification path for startup, one proxied request, and graceful shutdown before external testing begins.

fn-runSmoke.PURPOSE: Exercise startup, one proxy request, and shutdown over the implemented tunnel path.

M-BURST-DETECTOR-TESTS.NAME: BurstDetectorTests
M-BURST-DETECTOR-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for burst-detection thresholds, counters, and alert behavior.

fn-runBurstDetectorTests.PURPOSE: Verify burst-detection evidence and bounded alert behavior.

M-IROH-ADAPTER-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for the iroh adapter contract and cleanup-sensitive behavior.

fn-runIrohAdapterTests.PURPOSE: Verify iroh adapter contract expectations and cleanup guarantees.

M-PROXY-BRIDGE-TESTS.NAME: ProxyBridgeTests
M-PROXY-BRIDGE-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for bridge orchestration and transport-agnostic stream handling.

M-UDP-EGRESS-RELAY-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for UDP relay outbound, inbound, and reply-state behavior.

fn-runUdpRelayTests.PURPOSE: Verify governed UDP relay forwarding and return-state preservation.

M-SESSION-TESTS.NAME: SessionManagerTests
M-SESSION-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for top-level session orchestration behavior.

M-SESSION-EFFECTS-TESTS.NAME: SessionEffectsTests
M-SESSION-EFFECTS-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for typed session-effect and command-enum boundaries.

fn-runSessionEffectsTests.PURPOSE: Verify typed effect-surface invariants and bounded command enums.

M-SESSION-REGISTRY-TESTS.NAME: SessionRegistryTests
M-SESSION-REGISTRY-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for session-registry capacity and removal behavior.

fn-runSessionRegistryTests.PURPOSE: Verify reservation, insert, remove, and drain registry behavior.

M-SESSION-STATE-TESTS.NAME: SessionStateTests
M-SESSION-STATE-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for pure session-state transitions and emitted effects.

fn-runSessionStateTests.PURPOSE: Verify deterministic transition outcomes and ordered effect emission.

M-SESSION-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR-TESTS.NAME: SessionTransportSelectorTests
M-SESSION-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for sequential iroh-then-WSS transport fallback behavior.

fn-runSessionTransportSelectorTests.PURPOSE: Verify bounded transport selection, cancellation, and fallback outcomes.

M-SOCKS5-TESTS.NAME: Socks5Tests
M-SOCKS5-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for SOCKS5 handshake, request parsing, and reply behavior.

fn-runSocks5Tests.PURPOSE: Verify SOCKS5 request handling and exact reply semantics.

M-SOCKS5-REPLY-MAPPING-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for exhaustive SOCKS5 reply mapping behavior.

fn-runSocks5UdpAssociateTests.PURPOSE: Verify UDP ASSOCIATE relay binding and datagram handling behavior.

fn-runTlsTests.PURPOSE: Verify deterministic TLS loading and trust-anchor behavior.

M-DATAGRAM-CONTRACT-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for the shared datagram envelope and validation rules.

M-UDP-ASSOCIATION-REGISTRY-TESTS.NAME: UdpAssociationRegistryTests
M-UDP-ASSOCIATION-REGISTRY-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for UDP association ownership and lookup behavior.

fn-runUdpAssociationRegistryTests.PURPOSE: Verify association registration, lookup, and lifecycle behavior.

M-DATAGRAM-SESSION-MANAGER-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for datagram association setup and dispatch trajectory behavior.

fn-runDatagramSessionManagerTests.PURPOSE: Verify datagram manager association-open and dispatch-path behavior.

M-WSS-DATAGRAM-TESTS.NAME: WssDatagramGatewayTests
M-WSS-DATAGRAM-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for WSS datagram framing and runtime reply behavior.

fn-runWssDatagramTests.PURPOSE: Verify WSS datagram framing and reply-path behavior.

M-DATAGRAM-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for datagram-transport selection and outbound emission behavior.

fn-runDatagramTransportSelectorTests.PURPOSE: Verify datagram transport selection, timeout, and emission behavior.

M-WSS-GATEWAY-TESTS.NAME: WssGatewayTests
M-WSS-GATEWAY-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for WSS gateway stream, runtime, and transport-boundary behavior.

fn-runWssGatewayTests.PURPOSE: Verify WSS gateway contract, stream, and runtime datagram behavior.

M-UDP-ORIGDST-LINUX-TESTS.NAME: UdpOrigDstLinuxTests
M-UDP-ORIGDST-LINUX-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for Linux original-destination adapter behavior.

fn-runUdpOrigDstLinuxTests.PURPOSE: Verify Linux original-destination recovery adapter behavior.

M-UDP-ORIGDST-RUNTIME-TESTS.NAME: UdpOrigDstRuntimeTests
M-UDP-ORIGDST-RUNTIME-TESTS.purpose: Provide the governed module-local verification surface for repo-local original-destination runtime behavior.

fn-runUdpOrigDstRuntimeTests.PURPOSE: Verify original-destination runtime delivery and live-listener behavior.

M-LIVE-HOST-INVENTORY.NAME: LiveHostInventory
M-LIVE-HOST-INVENTORY.purpose: Freeze the two-host live-wave inventory including roles, paths, ports, and operator-only access assumptions.

fn-freezeHostRoles.PURPOSE: Assign one remote server host and one remote client host for the first live wave.

fn-recordPaths.PURPOSE: Pin binary, TLS, log, and socket paths on both hosts.

type-LiveHostInventoryRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the approved first-wave host inventory and invariants.

type-VerifiedHostRoles.PURPOSE: Current live inventory binds ghost-srv to 91.99.128.146 as server and ghost-cli to 178.104.104.208 as client.

M-LIVE-BUILD-PACK.NAME: LiveBuildPack
M-LIVE-BUILD-PACK.purpose: Define the release artifact, config payload, TLS assets, and staging layout used on both live hosts.

fn-pinReleaseBinary.PURPOSE: Freeze the deployed release artifact identity for both hosts.

fn-defineStagingLayout.PURPOSE: Define the on-host layout for binary, config, certs, and logs.

type-LiveBuildPackRecord.PURPOSE: Describes the approved live deployment bundle and staging shape.

type-LiveBuildChecksum.PURPOSE: Current first-wave binary checksum is 12fdeb6fd94b2835d8705936e1fe728edfb471d13faae837af5de506d5158ec2 on both hosts at /opt/n0wss/n0wss.

M-LIVE-TLS-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: LiveTlsBootstrap
M-LIVE-TLS-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Define the real TLS trust path for the first live WSS server and its client trust shape.

fn-pinServerTlsPaths.PURPOSE: Freeze server-side cert and key paths for the live wave.

fn-pinClientTrust.PURPOSE: Freeze the client trust material and endpoint identity.

type-LiveTlsBootstrapRecord.PURPOSE: Describes the approved live TLS layout and trust assumptions.

type-LiveTlsCertIdentity.PURPOSE: Current first-wave certificate is self-signed for IP 91.99.128.146 and DNS ghost-srv with matching client trust copy.

M-LIVE-SERVER-LAUNCH.NAME: LiveServerLaunch
M-LIVE-SERVER-LAUNCH.purpose: Define the remote server-mode launch procedure, readiness evidence, and shutdown surface for the live WSS host.
M-LIVE-SERVER-LAUNCH.depends: M-CLI, M-CONFIG, M-AUTH, M-TLS, M-WSS-GATEWAY, M-OBS, M-SESSION

fn-startServer.PURPOSE: Launch the live server-mode process on the remote host.

fn-captureServerReadiness.PURPOSE: Capture startup anchors, process identity, and bound-listener proof.

type-LiveLaunchEvidence.PURPOSE: Carries startup and socket evidence for a remote runtime launch.

type-LiveServerReadiness.PURPOSE: Current first-wave readiness confirms startup anchors and a bound listener on 0.0.0.0:7443 at 91.99.128.146.

M-LIVE-CLIENT-LAUNCH.NAME: LiveClientLaunch
M-LIVE-CLIENT-LAUNCH.purpose: Define the remote client-mode launch procedure, SOCKS5 readiness evidence, and remote endpoint targeting.
M-LIVE-CLIENT-LAUNCH.depends: M-CLI, M-CONFIG, M-SOCKS5, M-PROXY-BRIDGE, M-SESSION, M-OBS

fn-startClient.PURPOSE: Launch the live client-mode process on the remote host.

fn-captureClientReadiness.PURPOSE: Capture startup anchors, process identity, and bound SOCKS5 listener proof.

type-LiveLaunchEvidence.PURPOSE: Carries startup and socket evidence for a remote runtime launch.

type-LiveClientReadiness.PURPOSE: Current first-wave readiness confirms startup anchors and a bound SOCKS5 listener on 127.0.0.1:1080 at 178.104.104.208.

M-LIVE-PROXY-SMOKE.NAME: LiveProxySmoke
M-LIVE-PROXY-SMOKE.purpose: Define the first live end-to-end smoke path with one deterministic SOCKS5 request through the real two-host tunnel.

fn-runLiveSmoke.PURPOSE: Execute one deterministic live SOCKS5 request through the two-host tunnel.

fn-captureTrajectory.PURPOSE: Capture the minimum successful live trajectory across selection, reply, and pump.

type-LiveSmokeEvidence.PURPOSE: Carries output and trajectory evidence for the live smoke run.

type-LiveSmokeSuccess.PURPOSE: Current first-wave smoke returned Example Domain through socks5h://127.0.0.1:1080 on 178.104.104.208 with BLOCK_SELECT_TRANSPORT and BLOCK_PUMP_BIDIRECTIONAL evidence.

M-LIVE-OBS-EVIDENCE.NAME: LiveObservabilityEvidence
M-LIVE-OBS-EVIDENCE.purpose: Define the bounded logs, trace anchors, socket listings, and output artifacts that make the live wave debuggable.

fn-captureLogTails.PURPOSE: Capture bounded log tails from both live hosts.

fn-captureSocketState.PURPOSE: Capture listener and process state from both live hosts.

type-LiveEvidencePacket.PURPOSE: Carries the small high-signal evidence set for later triage.

type-LiveEvidenceSuccess.PURPOSE: Current first-wave evidence includes bounded server, client, and bad-auth log tails plus socket-state checks and smoke output metadata without raw secrets.

M-LIVE-FAILURE-TRIAGE.NAME: LiveFailureTriage
M-LIVE-FAILURE-TRIAGE.purpose: Define the failure-packet workflow for TLS, auth, transport, proxy reply, and shutdown failures in the first live wave.

fn-buildFailurePacket.PURPOSE: Condense live evidence into the canonical GRACE failure packet.

fn-routeNextAction.PURPOSE: Map the first divergent block to the next repair or rerun action.

type-FailurePacket.PURPOSE: Carries expected evidence, observed evidence, first divergent block, and next action.

type-LiveTriageOutcome.PURPOSE: Current first-wave triage closed the earlier binary, TLS, runtime, tracing, and WSS relay failures before the final green gate, so no new failure packet remained open.

M-DEPLOY-LAYOUT.NAME: DeployLayout
M-DEPLOY-LAYOUT.purpose: Define the canonical Linux deployment layout for binaries, certs, secrets, logs, and runtime state on managed hosts.

fn-defineFilesystemLayout.PURPOSE: Pin canonical host paths for binary, config, cert, log, and runtime-state assets.

fn-defineOwnershipModel.PURPOSE: Pin file ownership, permissions, and write boundaries for managed deployment.

type-DeployLayoutRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the approved managed deployment layout and host-role path invariants.

type-ManagedLayoutSurface.PURPOSE: The current managed layout fixes /opt/n0wss, /etc/systemd/system, and /var/log/n0wss-*.log as the canonical Phase-9 path family.

M-SECRET-HYGIENE.NAME: SecretHygiene
M-SECRET-HYGIENE.purpose: Define the managed secret-handling model so runtime auth material and operator credentials stay out of repository artifacts and reusable evidence.

fn-defineSecretInputs.PURPOSE: Pin the approved source classes for runtime secrets.

fn-defineRotationFollowUp.PURPOSE: Describe required secret cleanup and rotation actions after live testing.

type-SecretHandlingPolicy.PURPOSE: Carries the approved Phase-9 secret-placement and redaction rules.

type-ManagedSecretSources.PURPOSE: The current managed secret policy allows only host-local env files, transient operator env vars, and secure out-of-band delivery paths.

M-DEPLOY-SCRIPTING.NAME: DeployScripting
M-DEPLOY-SCRIPTING.purpose: Define a repeatable deployment and update workflow for server and client hosts rather than manual staging.

fn-deployServer.PURPOSE: Stage or update the managed server host under the canonical layout.

fn-deployClient.PURPOSE: Stage or update the managed client host under the canonical layout.

fn-printUsage.PURPOSE: Expose the governed CLI surface for managed deployment.

type-DeployRunResult.PURPOSE: Carries bounded artifact-switch and deploy evidence for one managed rollout.

M-SYSTEMD-UNITS.NAME: SystemdUnits
M-SYSTEMD-UNITS.purpose: Define managed systemd services for n0wss server and client roles with explicit startup, restart, and shutdown behavior.

fn-defineServerUnit.PURPOSE: Pin the managed service contract for the n0wss server role.

fn-defineClientUnit.PURPOSE: Pin the managed service contract for the n0wss client role.

type-SystemdUnitSet.PURPOSE: Carries the approved managed service names, env sources, and ExecStart invariants.

type-ManagedServiceLogTargets.PURPOSE: The current managed units append server and client runtime output to /var/log/n0wss-server.log and /var/log/n0wss-client.log.

M-LOG-ROTATION.NAME: LogRotation
M-LOG-ROTATION.purpose: Define bounded retention and rotation for n0wss logs so operators keep useful evidence without unbounded growth.

fn-defineRotationPolicy.PURPOSE: Pin retention count, rollover behavior, and active-log continuity guarantees.

type-LogRotationPolicy.PURPOSE: Carries the approved rotation behavior for managed n0wss logs.

type-ManagedLogRetention.PURPOSE: The current managed retention policy rotates the server, client, and bad-auth logs daily or earlier at 5M, retaining 7 compressed generations.

M-OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.NAME: OperatorRunbook
M-OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.purpose: Define the stable operator runbook for install, start, stop, update, rollback, and evidence capture in the managed deployment shape.

fn-documentInstall.PURPOSE: Describe the managed install and first-start procedure.

fn-documentRecovery.PURPOSE: Describe restart, rollback, and evidence-capture procedures.

type-OperatorRunbookRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the approved operator workflow for the managed deployment shape.

type-ManagedOperatorFlow.PURPOSE: The current runbook defines deploy-live, systemctl lifecycle, journalctl evidence capture, and bounded rollback as the canonical managed operator flow.

M-LIVE-WAVE-2.NAME: LiveWave2
M-LIVE-WAVE-2.purpose: Define the second live verification wave around service-managed startup, restart recovery, repeated smoke requests, and bounded reject-path evidence.

fn-runManagedSmoke.PURPOSE: Execute repeated service-managed smoke requests through the governed tunnel path.

fn-runRestartRecovery.PURPOSE: Exercise controlled service restart and reconnect recovery.

type-LiveWave2Evidence.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded evidence packets for managed success, restart, and bad-auth trajectories.

type-ManagedWave2Result.PURPOSE: The March 28, 2026 wave proved success, restart recovery, and redacted bad-auth rejection on systemd-managed ghost-srv and ghost-cli hosts.

M-TEST-GOVERNANCE-SYNC.NAME: TestGovernanceSync
M-TEST-GOVERNANCE-SYNC.purpose: Commit the prepared GRACE markup for substantive governed test files so release tagging no longer depends on a dirty worktree.
M-TEST-GOVERNANCE-SYNC.depends: M-AUTH, M-IROH-ADAPTER, M-OBS, M-SESSION, M-SOCKS5, M-WSS-GATEWAY

fn-syncTestHeaders.PURPOSE: Formalize the prepared test-file governance headers in committed source control.

type-GovernedTestFileSet.PURPOSE: Carries the 12 substantive test files whose markup must be committed before release.

M-RELEASE-MESSAGING.NAME: ReleaseMessaging
M-RELEASE-MESSAGING.purpose: Align README and operator-facing release copy with the completed managed deployment posture.

fn-syncReadme.PURPOSE: Remove stale release wording from README and align the release posture summary.

fn-syncOperatorCopy.PURPOSE: Keep operator-facing release copy aligned with the current repository state.

type-ReleaseMessagingRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the approved release-facing wording for the v0.3.0 preparation wave.

M-CHANGELOG-0-3-0.NAME: Changelog030
M-CHANGELOG-0-3-0.purpose: Prepare the v0.3.0 changelog entry so the next GitHub tag matches the implemented managed deployment scope.

fn-writeReleaseEntry.PURPOSE: Write the v0.3.0 changelog entry for the managed deployment and live-wave scope.

type-ChangelogRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the prepared 0.3.0 release notes for the next GitHub source release.

M-RELEASE-GATE-0-3-0.NAME: ReleaseGate030
M-RELEASE-GATE-0-3-0.purpose: Define the release gate that decides whether the repository is ready to tag v0.3.0.

fn-runReleaseGate.PURPOSE: Execute and summarize the release-prep checks before tag creation.

type-ReleaseGateResult.PURPOSE: Carries the release-ready or blocked decision with evidence.

M-GITHUB-RELEASE-0-3-0.NAME: GitHubRelease030
M-GITHUB-RELEASE-0-3-0.purpose: Align the existing GitHub source-release workflow with the next v0.3.0 tag and changelog entry.

fn-prepareTagPath.PURPOSE: Confirm that the existing tag workflow will publish the intended v0.3.0 release.

type-GitHubReleasePlan.PURPOSE: Carries the prepared source-release path for the next GitHub tag.

M-TELEGRAM-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramProfile
M-TELEGRAM-PROFILE.purpose: Define the supported Telegram-facing profile as standard SOCKS5 client compatibility over the existing n0wss tunnel path.

fn-documentTelegramProfile.PURPOSE: Describe the approved Telegram SOCKS5 profile and its explicit non-goals.

type-TelegramProxyProfile.PURPOSE: Carries the approved Telegram-facing SOCKS5 settings profile for n0wss.

M-TELEGRAM-READINESS.NAME: TelegramReadiness
M-TELEGRAM-READINESS.purpose: Define the operator path for exposing n0wss as a Telegram-facing SOCKS5 proxy and proving readiness before app-side testing.

fn-captureTelegramReady.PURPOSE: Collect the bounded readiness evidence before Telegram testing begins.

type-TelegramReadinessEvidence.PURPOSE: Carries the local SOCKS5 readiness packet used before Telegram app-side checks.

M-TELEGRAM-SMOKE.NAME: TelegramSmoke
M-TELEGRAM-SMOKE.purpose: Define the Telegram-specific compatibility wave for connect and reconnect behavior through the existing SOCKS5-over-tunnel path.

fn-runTelegramSmoke.PURPOSE: Execute the Telegram-specific connect and reconnect verification wave.

type-TelegramSmokeEvidence.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded connect and reconnect evidence packets for Telegram compatibility.

type-TelegramSmokeSuccess.PURPOSE: The current Telegram wave produced separate initial-connect and reconnect bursts on Telegram target IPs with SOCKS5 parse, transport-selection, bridge-pump, and accepted WSS handshake evidence.

M-TELEGRAM-EVIDENCE.NAME: TelegramEvidence
M-TELEGRAM-EVIDENCE.purpose: Define bounded success and failure evidence packets for Telegram-specific verification.

fn-captureTelegramEvidence.PURPOSE: Separate readiness, connect, reconnect, and failure packets for Telegram-specific debugging.

type-TelegramEvidencePacket.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded packet used to distinguish app-side and tunnel-side divergence.

type-TelegramLaunchDivergence.PURPOSE: The current Telegram wave preserves a local app-relaunch instability note separately from the successful reconnect packet so tunnel runtime evidence is not misclassified.

M-TELEGRAM-COMPAT.NAME: TelegramCompatAssessment
M-TELEGRAM-COMPAT.purpose: Bound the Telegram compatibility claim so documentation does not overstate unblock guarantees beyond verified evidence.

fn-documentCompatBounds.PURPOSE: Document what Telegram compatibility is proven and what remains unverified.

type-TelegramCompatRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded compatibility statement for Telegram usage over n0wss.

type-TelegramCompatEnvelope.PURPOSE: The current public claim surface is limited to Telegram Desktop compatibility through the governed SOCKS5 tunnel on the tested host setup and explicitly excludes universal unblock guarantees.

M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-RUNBOOK.NAME: TelegramForwardRunbook
M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-RUNBOOK.purpose: Document that the verified local Telegram Desktop path requires an SSH local forward from the operator workstation to the remote managed n0wss client listener.

fn-documentForwardedTelegramPath.PURPOSE: Describe when the local SSH forward is required for Telegram Desktop and when it is not.

type-TelegramForwardRunbookRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the clarified local-workstation versus remote-host operator path for Telegram.

M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-VERIFICATION.NAME: TelegramForwardVerification
M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-VERIFICATION.purpose: Align LV-007 and Telegram evidence requirements with the actual forwarded Desktop verification path.

fn-alignTelegramForwardVerification.PURPOSE: Add the SSH-forward prerequisite to the Telegram Desktop verification path.

type-TelegramForwardVerificationRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the corrected Telegram Desktop verification contract for forwarded local SOCKS5 access.

M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-GRAPH-SYNC.NAME: TelegramForwardGraphSync
M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-GRAPH-SYNC.purpose: Synchronize the graph with the clarified Telegram Desktop operator path without altering runtime architecture.

fn-syncTelegramForwardGraph.PURPOSE: Record the clarified forwarded Desktop operator path and its verification links.

type-TelegramForwardGraphSyncRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the graph-level synchronization record for the Telegram forward clarification wave.

type-TelegramForwardedDesktopPath.PURPOSE: The verified Desktop path is local Telegram Desktop -> local SSH-forwarded 127.0.0.1:1080 -> remote managed n0wss-client listener, not a directly local n0wss bind.

type-TelegramForwardLivenessTriage.PURPOSE: When Telegram stalls at Connecting on the local workstation, SSH-forward liveness is checked before classifying a tunnel-runtime defect.

M-CHANGELOG-0-3-1.NAME: Changelog031
M-CHANGELOG-0-3-1.purpose: Add the v0.3.1 changelog entry so the next GitHub tag matches the Telegram forward clarification patch scope closed after v0.3.0.

fn-writeReleaseEntry.PURPOSE: Write the v0.3.1 changelog entry for the Telegram forward clarification patch release.

type-ChangelogRecord031.PURPOSE: Carries the prepared 0.3.1 release notes for the next GitHub source release.

M-RELEASE-MESSAGING-0-3-1.NAME: ReleaseMessaging031
M-RELEASE-MESSAGING-0-3-1.purpose: Align release-facing documentation with the verified Telegram Desktop SSH-forward requirement before the v0.3.1 patch release.

fn-syncReleaseMessaging.PURPOSE: Keep release-facing wording aligned with the verified Telegram Desktop forwarded path.

type-ReleaseMessagingRecord031.PURPOSE: Carries the approved release-facing wording for the v0.3.1 patch-release wave.

M-RELEASE-GATE-0-3-1.NAME: ReleaseGate031
M-RELEASE-GATE-0-3-1.purpose: Define the release gate that decides whether the repository is ready to tag and push v0.3.1.

fn-runReleaseGate.PURPOSE: Execute and summarize the release-prep checks before the v0.3.1 tag is created.

type-ReleaseGateResult031.PURPOSE: Carries the release-ready or blocked decision for the v0.3.1 patch release.

M-GITHUB-RELEASE-0-3-1.NAME: GitHubRelease031
M-GITHUB-RELEASE-0-3-1.purpose: Align the existing GitHub source-release workflow with the v0.3.1 changelog and release gate outcome, then push the tag.

fn-prepareAndPushTagPath.PURPOSE: Confirm the v0.3.1 tag path and push the tag after the gate is green.

type-GitHubReleasePlan031.PURPOSE: Carries the prepared source-release path for the v0.3.1 tag.

M-FINAL-HOST-REBASELINE.NAME: FinalHostRebaseline
M-FINAL-HOST-REBASELINE.purpose: Confirm that both freshly rebuilt Ubuntu hosts are reachable, clean, and ready for the final controlled deployment wave.

fn-captureFreshHostBaseline.PURPOSE: Capture SSH, OS, and package-baseline evidence for both rebuilt hosts.

type-FinalHostBaselinePacket.PURPOSE: Carries the clean-host baseline packet used before final deployment.

M-FINAL-DEPLOY-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: FinalDeployBootstrap
M-FINAL-DEPLOY-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Redeploy the governed n0wss managed stack onto both fresh Ubuntu hosts without ad hoc mutation.

fn-runFinalDeploy.PURPOSE: Apply the approved managed deployment path to the rebuilt server and client hosts.

type-FinalDeployPacket.PURPOSE: Carries the final deployment packet proving the fresh hosts received the governed runtime layout.

M-FINAL-SERVICE-READINESS.NAME: FinalServiceReadiness
M-FINAL-SERVICE-READINESS.purpose: Prove server and client readiness on the rebuilt hosts before the operator Telegram Desktop acceptance begins.

fn-captureFinalReadiness.PURPOSE: Capture service, socket, and runtime-anchor evidence proving the rebuilt deployment is ready.

type-FinalServiceReadinessPacket.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded readiness packet used before the operator app-side handoff.

M-FINAL-TELEGRAM-HANDOFF.NAME: FinalTelegramHandoff
M-FINAL-TELEGRAM-HANDOFF.purpose: Define the exact operator handoff for the final Telegram Desktop test including the SSH forward and SOCKS5 settings.

fn-prepareFinalTelegramHandoff.PURPOSE: Prepare the exact SSH forward and SOCKS5 parameters the operator must use.

type-FinalTelegramHandoffPacket.PURPOSE: Carries the exact operator handoff instructions for the final Telegram Desktop acceptance wave.

M-FINAL-TELEGRAM-ACCEPTANCE.NAME: FinalTelegramAcceptance
M-FINAL-TELEGRAM-ACCEPTANCE.purpose: Run the final Telegram Desktop acceptance wave while bounded evidence is captured on both rebuilt hosts.

fn-runFinalTelegramAcceptance.PURPOSE: Coordinate the operator-led final Telegram Desktop wave with bounded evidence capture.

type-FinalTelegramAcceptancePacket.PURPOSE: Carries the final Telegram Desktop acceptance packet from the rebuilt environment.

M-FINAL-VOICE-AND-MEDIA.NAME: FinalVoiceAndMedia
M-FINAL-VOICE-AND-MEDIA.purpose: Record photos, large files, and call-path checks as separate media-heavy acceptance scenarios.

fn-runMediaAcceptance.PURPOSE: Record media-heavy Telegram scenarios as separable evidence packets.

type-FinalVoiceAndMediaPacket.PURPOSE: Carries the media-path acceptance results for the final Telegram Desktop wave.

M-FINAL-EVIDENCE-PACKET.NAME: FinalEvidencePacket
M-FINAL-EVIDENCE-PACKET.purpose: Assemble a bounded final acceptance packet from both hosts and the operator Telegram Desktop session without blind reruns.

fn-assembleFinalEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble success or failure packets with first-divergent-block discipline for the final live wave.

type-FinalEvidencePacketRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the bounded final acceptance packet for handoff and decision-making.

M-FINAL-GO-NO-GO.NAME: FinalGoNoGo
M-FINAL-GO-NO-GO.purpose: Produce the final go/no-go decision for the Telegram Desktop use case on the rebuilt two-host environment.

fn-decideFinalAcceptance.PURPOSE: Summarize the final acceptance evidence into a bounded go/no-go decision for the operator.

type-FinalDecisionRecord.PURPOSE: Carries the final bounded decision for the Telegram Desktop use case on the rebuilt hosts.

M-CHANGELOG-0-3-2.purpose: Prepare the v0.3.2 changelog entry for the current verified local baseline without overstating Telegram call support.

M-RELEASED-MASTER-TARGET.purpose: Freeze the corrective origin/master sync target as the released v0.3.2 commit rather than the newer local HEAD.

M-RELEASED-MASTER-GATE.purpose: Gate the push of origin/master specifically to the released v0.3.2 commit.

M-GITHUB-MASTER-SYNC-032.purpose: Push v0.3.2 to origin/master so GitHub default-branch browsing becomes a copy of the released stable baseline.

M-DATAGRAM-CONTRACT.NAME: DatagramContract
M-DATAGRAM-CONTRACT.purpose: Define the shared transport-agnostic datagram envelope, association identity, and bounded UDP error surface.

fn-datagramEnvelope.PURPOSE: Represent one normalized UDP datagram plus target and association metadata.

M-SOCKS5-UDP-ASSOCIATE.NAME: Socks5UdpAssociate
M-SOCKS5-UDP-ASSOCIATE.purpose: Add SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE ingress and governed UDP relay normalization alongside the existing CONNECT path.

fn-handleUdpAssociate.PURPOSE: Negotiate SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE and return the governed relay bind.

M-UDP-ASSOCIATION-REGISTRY.NAME: UdpAssociationRegistry
M-UDP-ASSOCIATION-REGISTRY.purpose: Track active UDP associations, relay metadata, idle timers, and deterministic cleanup.

M-DATAGRAM-SESSION-MANAGER.NAME: DatagramSessionManager
M-DATAGRAM-SESSION-MANAGER.purpose: Coordinate UDP association lifecycle and datagram dispatch across the tunnel boundary.

fn-openAssociation.PURPOSE: Open one datagram association under the governed session rules.

M-WSS-DATAGRAM-GATEWAY.NAME: WssDatagramGateway
M-WSS-DATAGRAM-GATEWAY.purpose: Encapsulate governed datagrams in WSS frames without changing the existing stream tunnel contract.

fn-sendDatagram.PURPOSE: Carry one datagram over the WSS control path.

M-UDP-EGRESS-RELAY.NAME: UdpEgressRelay
M-UDP-EGRESS-RELAY.purpose: Relay real UDP traffic on the server side and return inbound packets to the owning association.

fn-relayOutboundDatagram.PURPOSE: Send one outbound UDP datagram to the remote target.

M-DATAGRAM-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR.NAME: DatagramTransportSelector
M-DATAGRAM-TRANSPORT-SELECTOR.purpose: Select the governed UDP-capable transport path for datagrams, initially with the WSS-backed datagram route.

fn-selectTransport.PURPOSE: Resolve one datagram transport path for a governed UDP association.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramCallsProfile
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-PROFILE.purpose: Document the bounded Telegram Desktop calls support claim for the governed UDP-capable path.

fn-documentTelegramCallsProfile.PURPOSE: Describe the bounded Telegram Desktop calls support envelope.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-WAVE.NAME: TelegramCallsWave
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-WAVE.purpose: Run a dedicated Telegram Desktop calls verification wave over the UDP-capable path.

fn-runTelegramCallsWave.PURPOSE: Execute one governed Telegram voice or video call verification wave.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-EVIDENCE.NAME: TelegramCallsEvidence
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-EVIDENCE.purpose: Capture bounded setup, media, reconnect, and failure packets for Telegram calls verification.

fn-captureTelegramCallsEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble bounded evidence packets for Telegram calls.

M-CALLS-HOST-REBASELINE.NAME: CallsHostRebaseline
M-CALLS-HOST-REBASELINE.purpose: Confirm that both fresh Ubuntu hosts are reachable and suitable for the controlled live Telegram calls deploy wave.

fn-captureCallsHostBaseline.PURPOSE: Capture fresh-host identity and baseline tooling evidence for the live calls wave.

M-CALLS-DEPLOY-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: CallsDeployBootstrap
M-CALLS-DEPLOY-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Deploy the UDP-capable n0wss baseline onto the fresh host pair for the live Telegram calls wave.

fn-deployCallsBaseline.PURPOSE: Deploy the approved UDP-capable build to the live client and server hosts.

M-CALLS-SERVICE-READINESS.NAME: CallsServiceReadiness
M-CALLS-SERVICE-READINESS.purpose: Prove that the live client and server are ready for Telegram calls, including UDP-capable runtime markers.

fn-captureCallsReadiness.PURPOSE: Collect service-manager and runtime evidence before operator handoff.

M-CALLS-HANDOFF.NAME: CallsHandoff
M-CALLS-HANDOFF.purpose: Prepare one exact operator path for Telegram Desktop calls, including SSH-forward and SOCKS5 settings.

fn-prepareCallsHandoff.PURPOSE: Prepare the exact operator instructions for the live calls wave.

M-CALLS-VOICE-WAVE.NAME: CallsVoiceWave
M-CALLS-VOICE-WAVE.purpose: Run one bounded Telegram voice-call live wave with separate signaling and media evidence.

fn-runCallsVoiceWave.PURPOSE: Coordinate the operator voice-call attempt and bounded UDP evidence capture.

M-CALLS-VIDEO-WAVE.NAME: CallsVideoWave
M-CALLS-VIDEO-WAVE.purpose: Run a separate Telegram video-call live wave so video behavior is not conflated with the voice result.

fn-runCallsVideoWave.PURPOSE: Coordinate the operator video-call attempt and bounded UDP evidence capture.

M-CALLS-RECONNECT-WAVE.NAME: CallsReconnectWave
M-CALLS-RECONNECT-WAVE.purpose: Prove that a second call attempt opens a fresh UDP association after the first call ends or idles out.

fn-runCallsReconnectWave.PURPOSE: Run one fresh reconnect call attempt and capture association-lifecycle evidence.

M-CALLS-EVIDENCE-PACKET.NAME: CallsEvidencePacket
M-CALLS-EVIDENCE-PACKET.purpose: Assemble separate live packets for call setup, media flow, reconnect, and failure triage.

fn-captureCallsEvidencePacket.PURPOSE: Capture the bounded packet set for the live Telegram calls wave.

M-CALLS-GO-NO-GO.NAME: CallsGoNoGo
M-CALLS-GO-NO-GO.purpose: Issue the bounded go-or-no-go decision for Telegram Desktop voice and video calls on the tested live setup.

fn-issueCallsDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the final bounded calls support decision from the live evidence packets.

M-CALLS-RERUN-DEPLOY.NAME: CallsRerunDeploy
M-CALLS-RERUN-DEPLOY.purpose: Redeploy the repaired UDP ASSOCIATE ingress baseline to the Telegram calls test hosts before a post-fix rerun.

fn-deployCallsRerunBaseline.PURPOSE: Redeploy the repaired live baseline for the Telegram calls rerun wave.

M-CALLS-RERUN-READINESS.NAME: CallsRerunReadiness
M-CALLS-RERUN-READINESS.purpose: Prove post-fix service state and UDP ASSOCIATE ingress readiness before another operator call attempt.

fn-captureCallsRerunReadiness.PURPOSE: Capture post-fix readiness and UDP ingress evidence for the rerun wave.

M-CALLS-RERUN-VOICE.NAME: CallsRerunVoice

M-CALLS-RERUN-VIDEO.NAME: CallsRerunVideo
M-CALLS-RERUN-VIDEO.purpose: Repeat a separate Telegram video-call wave after the UDP ingress fix without inheriting the voice result.

fn-runCallsRerunVideo.PURPOSE: Run the repaired live video-call rerun with a separate evidence packet.

M-CALLS-RERUN-EVIDENCE.NAME: CallsRerunEvidence
M-CALLS-RERUN-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the post-fix deploy, readiness, voice, video, and failure packets for the Telegram calls rerun wave.

M-CALLS-RERUN-DECISION.purpose: Issue the updated bounded Telegram calls decision after the repaired rerun wave.

M-LOCAL-CLIENT-RUNBOOK.NAME: LocalClientRunbook
M-LOCAL-CLIENT-RUNBOOK.purpose: Define the operator runbook for a truly local n0wss-client calls path without SSH port forwarding.

fn-prepareLocalClientRunbook.PURPOSE: Document the exact local-client operator path for Telegram Desktop calls.

M-LOCAL-CLIENT-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: LocalClientBootstrap
M-LOCAL-CLIENT-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Define the bounded bootstrap shape for running n0wss-client locally on the Telegram Desktop workstation.

M-LOCAL-CALLS-READINESS.NAME: LocalCallsReadiness

M-LOCAL-CALLS-EVIDENCE.NAME: LocalCallsEvidence
M-LOCAL-CALLS-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the local-topology readiness, voice, video, failure, and comparison packets for Telegram calls verification.

M-LOCAL-CALLS-GO-NO-GO.NAME: LocalCallsGoNoGo
M-LOCAL-CALLS-GO-NO-GO.purpose: Issue the bounded Telegram calls decision for the local-client topology on the tested setup.

M-CALLS-FAILURE-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the bounded post-Phase-21 hypothesis set for Telegram calls failure before deeper diagnosis starts.

M-CALLS-UDP-PROBE-TOOLING.NAME: CallsUdpProbeTooling
M-CALLS-UDP-PROBE-TOOLING.purpose: Prepare a controlled UDP ASSOCIATE and datagram probe path outside Telegram UI.

M-CALLS-LIVE-PACKET-CAPTURE.NAME: CallsLivePacketCapture
M-CALLS-LIVE-PACKET-CAPTURE.purpose: Add bounded workstation and remote packet-capture evidence for Telegram calls attempts.

fn-captureCallsPackets.PURPOSE: Capture bounded workstation and remote packet evidence during calls attempts.

M-CALLS-APP-BEHAVIOR-CLASSIFIER.NAME: CallsAppBehaviorClassifier
M-CALLS-APP-BEHAVIOR-CLASSIFIER.purpose: Classify whether Telegram Desktop uses the governed media path, bypasses it, or stalls before media begins.

fn-classifyTelegramCallsBehavior.PURPOSE: Classify Telegram Desktop media-path behavior on the tested setup.

M-CALLS-REMOTE-MEDIA-PROBE.NAME: CallsRemoteMediaProbe
M-CALLS-REMOTE-MEDIA-PROBE.purpose: Probe the remote UDP relay boundary with controlled datagrams independently of Telegram UI state.

fn-probeRemoteMediaBoundary.PURPOSE: Probe remote UDP media reachability through the governed relay path.

M-CALLS-BLOCKING-BOUNDARY.NAME: CallsBlockingBoundary
M-CALLS-BLOCKING-BOUNDARY.purpose: Classify the remaining calls blocker as app behavior mismatch, datagram-path defect, or plausible external filtering only after controlled evidence is complete.

fn-classifyBlockingBoundary.PURPOSE: Classify the remaining Telegram calls blocker without premature provider blame.

M-CALLS-DECISION-NEXT-ARCH.NAME: CallsDecisionNextArch
M-CALLS-DECISION-NEXT-ARCH.purpose: Issue the bounded architectural next-step decision after the Telegram calls root-cause isolation wave.

fn-issueCallsNextArchDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the next architectural decision for Telegram calls based on bounded isolation evidence.

M-DATAGRAM-ROUNDTRIP-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: DatagramRoundTripHypothesis
M-DATAGRAM-ROUNDTRIP-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the bounded controlled datagram round-trip hypothesis set after the Telegram calls root-cause wave.

fn-freezeRoundTripHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the bounded outbound and inbound datagram hypothesis set.

M-DATAGRAM-ECHO-TARGET.NAME: DatagramEchoTarget
M-DATAGRAM-ECHO-TARGET.purpose: Provide a bounded remote UDP echo target with explicit startup, capture, and cleanup surfaces.

fn-prepareEchoTarget.PURPOSE: Prepare and bound the remote UDP echo target used for controlled probes.

M-DATAGRAM-OUTBOUND-TRACE.NAME: DatagramOutboundTrace
M-DATAGRAM-OUTBOUND-TRACE.purpose: Trace the controlled outbound datagram path from local UDP ASSOCIATE through remote echo-target ingress.

fn-traceDatagramOutbound.PURPOSE: Trace and classify the outbound controlled datagram trajectory.

M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-TRACE.NAME: DatagramInboundTrace
M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-TRACE.purpose: Trace the inbound controlled datagram reply from the remote echo target back to the local probe surface.

fn-traceDatagramInbound.PURPOSE: Trace and classify the inbound controlled datagram reply path.

M-DATAGRAM-CAPTURE-CORRELATION.NAME: DatagramCaptureCorrelation
M-DATAGRAM-CAPTURE-CORRELATION.purpose: Correlate local probe output, runtime markers, and remote capture into one bounded datagram packet.

fn-correlateDatagramEvidence.PURPOSE: Correlate local and remote evidence for one controlled datagram attempt.

M-DATAGRAM-BLOCKING-BOUNDARY.NAME: DatagramBlockingBoundary
M-DATAGRAM-BLOCKING-BOUNDARY.purpose: Name the first unresolved controlled datagram layer without reopening Telegram UI ambiguity.

fn-classifyDatagramBoundary.PURPOSE: Classify the first unresolved controlled datagram layer.

M-DATAGRAM-NEXT-STEP-DECISION.NAME: DatagramNextStepDecision
M-DATAGRAM-NEXT-STEP-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded next-step decision after the controlled datagram round-trip isolation wave.

fn-issueDatagramNextStepDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the next-step decision from the datagram round-trip boundary packet.

M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: DatagramRepairHypothesis
M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the bounded datagram repair scope after Phase-23 so the next wave stays between local ingress and proven remote ingress.

fn-freezeDatagramRepairHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the bounded datagram-path repair hypothesis after the round-trip isolation packet.

M-DATAGRAM-DISPATCH-INSTRUMENTATION.NAME: DatagramDispatchInstrumentation
M-DATAGRAM-DISPATCH-INSTRUMENTATION.purpose: Strengthen bounded dispatch-side anchors between local UDP ingress, datagram manager dispatch, and transport selection.

fn-instrumentDatagramDispatch.PURPOSE: Emit deeper bounded markers across local datagram dispatch transitions.

M-DATAGRAM-OUTBOUND-DISPATCH-REPAIR.NAME: DatagramOutboundDispatchRepair
M-DATAGRAM-OUTBOUND-DISPATCH-REPAIR.purpose: Repair the local handoff from governed UDP relay ingress to outbound datagram dispatch.

fn-repairOutboundDispatch.PURPOSE: Repair the manager-owned outbound dispatch path after local UDP ingress.

M-WSS-DATAGRAM-EMIT-REPAIR.NAME: WssDatagramEmitRepair
M-WSS-DATAGRAM-EMIT-REPAIR.purpose: Repair or narrow the WSS datagram emission path so outbound datagrams actually leave the client runtime.

fn-repairWssDatagramEmit.PURPOSE: Repair the client-side datagram emission path across the WSS carrier.

M-UDP-RELAY-OUTBOUND-REPAIR.NAME: UdpRelayOutboundRepair
M-UDP-RELAY-OUTBOUND-REPAIR.purpose: Repair the server-side UDP relay until outbound datagrams become proven remote echo-target ingress.

fn-repairUdpRelayOutbound.PURPOSE: Repair the server-side outbound relay path to the remote echo target.

M-DATAGRAM-ROUNDTRIP-RERUN.NAME: DatagramRoundTripRerun
M-DATAGRAM-ROUNDTRIP-RERUN.purpose: Replay the controlled datagram probe after repairs and classify the new deepest proven layer.

fn-rerunDatagramRoundTrip.PURPOSE: Replay one bounded controlled probe after datagram-path repairs.

M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-EVIDENCE.NAME: DatagramRepairEvidence
M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the pre-fix versus post-fix comparison packet for the repaired datagram path.

fn-assembleDatagramRepairEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the bounded datagram repair comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-DECISION.NAME: DatagramRepairDecision
M-DATAGRAM-REPAIR-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded post-repair decision on whether the project can return to Telegram-specific verification.

fn-issueDatagramRepairDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision after the datagram repair comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: DatagramRuntimeHypothesis
M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the bounded runtime-glue blocker so the next wave targets missing live callers instead of reopening already-repaired helper-level work.

fn-freezeDatagramRuntimeHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the bounded runtime-glue hypothesis after the stopped Phase-24 wave.

M-SOCKS5-UDP-RUNTIME-LOOP.NAME: Socks5UdpRuntimeLoop
M-SOCKS5-UDP-RUNTIME-LOOP.purpose: Add the live local UDP receive loop after UDP ASSOCIATE so governed relay packets enter the repaired manager handoff path.

fn-runSocks5UdpRuntimeLoop.PURPOSE: Run the live UDP relay receive loop after SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE succeeds.

M-WSS-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-CARRIER.NAME: WssDatagramRuntimeCarrier
M-WSS-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-CARRIER.purpose: Implement the production WSS datagram carrier over the live websocket runtime.

fn-runWssDatagramRuntimeCarrier.PURPOSE: Emit and receive governed datagram frames through the real WSS runtime.

M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-BRIDGE.NAME: DatagramRuntimeBridge
M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-BRIDGE.purpose: Bind the live local UDP runtime loop, manager handoff, and production carrier emit path into one bounded runtime bridge.

fn-bridgeRuntimeDatagramPath.PURPOSE: Bridge live local UDP ingress into the production datagram carrier path.

M-SERVER-DATAGRAM-INGRESS.NAME: ServerDatagramIngress
M-SERVER-DATAGRAM-INGRESS.purpose: Add the server-side runtime receive path for WSS datagram frames and invoke relay outbound handling.

fn-runServerDatagramIngress.PURPOSE: Receive governed datagram frames on the server runtime and invoke relay outbound handling.

M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-RERUN.NAME: DatagramRuntimeRerun
M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-RERUN.purpose: Replay the controlled datagram probe through the full live runtime glue path after wiring is complete.

fn-rerunDatagramRuntimeProbe.PURPOSE: Replay one bounded datagram probe through the real runtime glue path.

M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-EVIDENCE.NAME: DatagramRuntimeEvidence
M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the bounded post-runtime-glue packet and compare it against the earlier datagram boundaries.

fn-assembleDatagramRuntimeEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the bounded runtime-glue comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-DECISION.NAME: DatagramRuntimeDecision
M-DATAGRAM-RUNTIME-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded post-runtime-glue decision about whether Telegram-specific verification is finally justified.

fn-issueDatagramRuntimeDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision after the runtime-glue comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: DatagramInboundHypothesis
M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the inbound-only blocker after Phase-25 so the next wave repairs inbound reply return rather than reopening proven outbound runtime glue.

fn-freezeDatagramInboundHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the bounded inbound-return hypothesis after Phase-25.

M-PHASE24-SUPERSEDE.NAME: Phase24Supersede
M-PHASE24-SUPERSEDE.purpose: Explicitly supersede the stale pending tail of Phase-24 so execution truth no longer queues the older repair rerun path.

fn-supersedePhase24Tail.PURPOSE: Mark the stale Phase-24 tail as superseded across the shared artifacts.

M-DATAGRAM-RELAY-STATE.NAME: DatagramRelayState
M-DATAGRAM-RELAY-STATE.purpose: Retain server-side association context so inbound UDP replies can be mapped back to the correct governed datagram association.

fn-recordDatagramRelayState.PURPOSE: Retain bounded association and peer-return state for inbound datagram mapping.

M-SERVER-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-RECEIVE.NAME: ServerDatagramInboundReceive
M-SERVER-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-RECEIVE.purpose: Add the server-side receive loop for inbound reply datagrams after relay outbound is already proven.

fn-receiveServerDatagramReply.PURPOSE: Read and normalize one inbound UDP reply on the server runtime.

M-WSS-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-EMIT.NAME: WssDatagramInboundEmit
M-WSS-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-EMIT.purpose: Emit one inbound datagram back over the governed WSS datagram path from server runtime to client runtime.

fn-emitInboundDatagram.PURPOSE: Return one server-side inbound datagram through the production WSS carrier.

M-CLIENT-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-BRIDGE.NAME: ClientDatagramInboundBridge
M-CLIENT-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-BRIDGE.purpose: Bridge one returned WSS datagram back into the local UDP relay socket that owns the original association.

fn-deliverInboundDatagram.PURPOSE: Deliver one bounded inbound datagram from client runtime to local UDP relay ownership.

M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-RERUN.NAME: DatagramInboundRerun
M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-RERUN.purpose: Replay the controlled datagram probe after inbound-return repairs and classify whether reply delivery is finally proven.

fn-rerunDatagramInboundProbe.PURPOSE: Replay one bounded probe focused on inbound reply return.

M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-EVIDENCE.NAME: DatagramInboundEvidence
M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the post-inbound-repair packet and compare it against the earlier datagram baselines.

fn-assembleDatagramInboundEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the bounded inbound-return comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-DECISION.NAME: DatagramInboundDecision
M-DATAGRAM-INBOUND-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded post-inbound-repair decision on whether a Telegram-specific wave is finally justified.

fn-issueDatagramInboundDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision after the inbound-return comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: DatagramServerInboundHypothesis
M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the new bounded blocker after Phase-26 so the next wave repairs server-side inbound receive and return emission instead of reopening outbound glue.

fn-freezeServerInboundBoundary.PURPOSE: Freeze the new server-inbound-only repair boundary after Phase-26.

M-SERVER-RELAY-SESSION-STATE.NAME: ServerRelaySessionState
M-SERVER-RELAY-SESSION-STATE.purpose: Retain enough server-side relay session state for inbound replies to map back to the owning datagram association.

fn-retainRelaySessionState.PURPOSE: Retain server-side relay state long enough for inbound reply receive and return mapping.

M-SERVER-INBOUND-RECEIVE-LOOP.NAME: ServerInboundReceiveLoop
M-SERVER-INBOUND-RECEIVE-LOOP.purpose: Add the real server-side inbound UDP receive loop after proven outbound relay.

fn-readServerInboundReply.PURPOSE: Read one inbound UDP reply for the owning datagram association on the server runtime.

M-SERVER-INBOUND-SOURCE-VALIDATION.NAME: ServerInboundSourceValidation
M-SERVER-INBOUND-SOURCE-VALIDATION.purpose: Classify valid inbound replies versus foreign-source or wrong-association packets deterministically.

fn-validateInboundReplySource.PURPOSE: Validate one server-side inbound reply source against the retained relay session state.

M-WSS-SERVER-RETURN-EMIT.NAME: WssServerReturnEmit
M-WSS-SERVER-RETURN-EMIT.purpose: Emit one validated inbound datagram reply back over the governed WSS carrier from server runtime to client runtime.

fn-emitServerInboundReturn.PURPOSE: Emit one validated server-side inbound reply back over the WSS datagram carrier.

M-CLIENT-INBOUND-RETURN-RERUN.NAME: ClientInboundReturnRerun
M-CLIENT-INBOUND-RETURN-RERUN.purpose: Replay the bounded controlled probe after server-side inbound-return repairs and classify whether inbound delivery now reaches the client relay socket.

fn-rerunInboundProbe.PURPOSE: Replay one bounded UDP probe against the repaired server-side inbound-return path.

M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-EVIDENCE.NAME: DatagramServerInboundEvidence
M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the comparison packet against the completed Phase-25 and Phase-26 datagram baselines after server-side inbound repairs.

fn-assembleServerInboundEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the bounded server-side inbound-return comparison packet.

M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-DECISION.NAME: DatagramServerInboundDecision
M-DATAGRAM-SERVER-INBOUND-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded post-server-inbound-repair decision on whether a Telegram-specific wave is finally justified.

fn-issueServerInboundDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision after the server-side inbound-return comparison packet.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-ROUNDTRIP-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramCallsRoundTripProfile
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-ROUNDTRIP-PROFILE.purpose: Freeze the post-Phase-27 truth-boundary so Telegram calls reruns no longer reopen generic datagram transport diagnosis.

fn-freezeTelegramCallsRoundTripProfile.PURPOSE: Freeze the post-roundtrip Telegram calls evidence boundary before the next app-facing wave.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-READINESS.NAME: TelegramCallsRerunReadiness
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-READINESS.purpose: Prove fresh readiness and a green controlled datagram round-trip in the same deployment window before the Telegram rerun wave starts.

fn-verifyTelegramCallsRerunReadiness.PURPOSE: Verify the same live window still has active services, local SOCKS5 bind, and a green controlled probe.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-VOICE-RERUN.NAME: TelegramCallsVoiceRerun
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-VOICE-RERUN.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram Desktop voice-call wave after the green datagram round-trip baseline.

fn-rerunTelegramVoiceCall.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram Desktop voice-call rerun after transport green evidence.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-VIDEO-RERUN.NAME: TelegramCallsVideoRerun
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-VIDEO-RERUN.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram Desktop video-call wave after the green datagram round-trip baseline without conflating it with the voice result.

fn-rerunTelegramVideoCall.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram Desktop video-call rerun on the same green transport baseline.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-APP-CLASSIFIER.NAME: TelegramCallsAppClassifier
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-APP-CLASSIFIER.purpose: Classify the new Telegram calls outcome as governed media success, direct-media behavior, or signaling-only stall without reopening transport diagnosis.

fn-classifyTelegramCallsAppOutcome.PURPOSE: Classify the post-roundtrip Telegram call outcome as app-facing rather than transport-facing.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RECONNECT-RERUN.NAME: TelegramCallsReconnectRerun
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RECONNECT-RERUN.purpose: Replay one fresh second Telegram call attempt after the first rerun and keep reconnect evidence separate.

fn-rerunTelegramReconnect.PURPOSE: Run one bounded reconnect call attempt after the first post-roundtrip rerun.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-EVIDENCE.NAME: TelegramCallsRerunEvidence
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the bounded readiness, voice, video, reconnect, and comparison packets for the Telegram-specific rerun wave after Phase-27.

fn-assembleTelegramCallsRerunEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the post-roundtrip Telegram calls evidence packet and compare it with earlier no-go waves.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-DECISION.NAME: TelegramCallsRerunDecision
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-RERUN-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded post-Phase-27 Telegram calls decision without widening claims beyond the tested Desktop setup.

fn-issueTelegramCallsRerunDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision for the Telegram calls rerun after transport green evidence.

M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: TelegramMediaHypothesis
M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the post-Phase-28 truth-boundary where controlled transport is green but Telegram calls still fail at an app-facing media layer on the tested Desktop setup.

fn-freezeTelegramMediaHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the post-Phase-28 Telegram-specific media boundary above the green transport baseline.

M-TELEGRAM-DESKTOP-HANDOFF-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramDesktopHandoffProfile
M-TELEGRAM-DESKTOP-HANDOFF-PROFILE.purpose: Fix the exact Telegram Desktop proxy handoff profile for the next bounded media-behavior wave.

fn-freezeTelegramDesktopHandoff.PURPOSE: Freeze the exact Desktop proxy handoff shape for post-Phase-28 media diagnostics.

M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-CAPTURE.NAME: TelegramMediaCapture
M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-CAPTURE.purpose: Define the bounded capture surface around Telegram Desktop calls so later media packets are comparable and deterministic.

fn-prepareTelegramMediaCapture.PURPOSE: Prepare bounded capture guidance around Desktop handoff, WSS transport, and app-facing media behavior.

M-TELEGRAM-VOICE-MEDIA-CLASSIFIER.NAME: TelegramVoiceMediaClassifier
M-TELEGRAM-VOICE-MEDIA-CLASSIFIER.purpose: Classify the post-answer voice-call outcome above the green datagram baseline as signaling-only stall, governed-media progress, or other app-facing behavior.

M-TELEGRAM-VIDEO-MEDIA-CLASSIFIER.NAME: TelegramVideoMediaClassifier
M-TELEGRAM-VIDEO-MEDIA-CLASSIFIER.purpose: Classify the bounded video-call outcome separately from voice and detect whether video diverges earlier or at the same app-facing layer.

fn-classifyTelegramVideoMedia.PURPOSE: Classify the bounded video-call media behavior without inheriting the voice result.

M-TELEGRAM-PROXY-BEHAVIOR-COMPARISON.NAME: TelegramProxyBehaviorComparison
M-TELEGRAM-PROXY-BEHAVIOR-COMPARISON.purpose: Compare the new voice and video media classifiers against the green baseline and older calls packets to isolate Telegram-specific proxy behavior.

fn-compareTelegramProxyBehavior.PURPOSE: Compare the new bounded media packets against the green transport baseline and older no-go waves.

M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-EVIDENCE.NAME: TelegramMediaEvidence
M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the bounded handoff, capture, classifier, and comparison packets for the Telegram-specific media-behavior wave.

fn-assembleTelegramMediaEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the Telegram-specific media evidence packet above the green transport baseline.

M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-DECISION.NAME: TelegramMediaDecision
M-TELEGRAM-MEDIA-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded next-step decision for the tested Telegram Desktop setup after the Telegram-specific media-behavior wave.

fn-issueTelegramMediaDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded next-step decision after Telegram-specific media comparison.

M-TELEGRAM-WORKAROUND-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: TelegramWorkaroundHypothesis
M-TELEGRAM-WORKAROUND-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the post-Phase-29 truth-boundary where transport remains green but the tested Telegram Desktop setup still needs a Telegram-specific workaround or alternate topology.

fn-freezeTelegramWorkaroundHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the workaround-only boundary above the completed Phase-29 media packet.

M-ALTERNATE-TOPOLOGY-PROFILE.NAME: AlternateTopologyProfile
M-ALTERNATE-TOPOLOGY-PROFILE.purpose: Freeze one bounded alternate app topology for the Telegram workaround wave.

fn-freezeAlternateTopology.PURPOSE: Freeze one bounded alternate app topology distinct from the exact Phase-29 Desktop route.

M-TELEGRAM-WORKAROUND-CAPTURE.NAME: TelegramWorkaroundCapture
M-TELEGRAM-WORKAROUND-CAPTURE.purpose: Define the bounded capture packet for the workaround wave so alternate-topology results stay comparable with Phase-29.

fn-prepareTelegramWorkaroundCapture.PURPOSE: Prepare the workaround capture packet for alternate-topology Telegram media diagnostics.

M-ALTERNATE-VOICE-WAVE.NAME: AlternateVoiceWave
M-ALTERNATE-VOICE-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram voice-call attempt on the alternate topology and classify whether the no-go class changes.

fn-runAlternateVoiceWave.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram voice-call attempt on the alternate topology.

M-ALTERNATE-VIDEO-WAVE.NAME: AlternateVideoWave
M-ALTERNATE-VIDEO-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram video-call attempt on the alternate topology without inheriting the voice result.

fn-runAlternateVideoWave.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram video-call attempt on the alternate topology.

M-WORKAROUND-BEHAVIOR-COMPARISON.NAME: WorkaroundBehaviorComparison
M-WORKAROUND-BEHAVIOR-COMPARISON.purpose: Compare alternate-topology media packets against the completed Phase-29 packet and determine whether topology changes the Telegram no-go class.

fn-compareWorkaroundBehavior.PURPOSE: Compare alternate-topology media packets against the completed Phase-29 packet.

M-WORKAROUND-EVIDENCE.NAME: WorkaroundEvidence
M-WORKAROUND-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the bounded alternate-topology handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison packets for the workaround wave.

fn-assembleWorkaroundEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the workaround evidence packet above the preserved green transport baseline.

M-WORKAROUND-DECISION.NAME: WorkaroundDecision
M-WORKAROUND-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded workaround decision for the tested Telegram setup after the alternate-topology wave.

fn-issueWorkaroundDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded alternate-topology workaround decision for the tested Telegram setup.

M-TELEGRAM-DEEP-WORKAROUND-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: TelegramDeepWorkaroundHypothesis
M-TELEGRAM-DEEP-WORKAROUND-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the post-Phase-30 truth-boundary where future work stays strictly inside Telegram-specific workaround space above the preserved green transport baseline.

fn-freezeTelegramDeepWorkaroundHypothesi.PURPOSE: Freeze the deeper-workaround-only boundary above the completed Phase-30 packet.

M-TELEGRAM-APP-VARIANT-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramAppVariantProfile
M-TELEGRAM-APP-VARIANT-PROFILE.purpose: Freeze one bounded Telegram-specific app variant for the next workaround wave.

fn-freezeTelegramAppVariant.PURPOSE: Freeze one bounded Telegram-specific app variant distinct from earlier topology-only packets.

M-TELEGRAM-DEEP-CAPTURE.NAME: TelegramDeepCapture
M-TELEGRAM-DEEP-CAPTURE.purpose: Define the bounded capture packet for the Telegram-specific app variant so it stays directly comparable with the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 packets.

fn-prepareTelegramDeepCapture.PURPOSE: Prepare the deeper-workaround capture packet for the chosen Telegram app variant.

M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-VOICE-WAVE.NAME: TelegramVariantVoiceWave
M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-VOICE-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram voice-call attempt on the chosen app variant and classify whether the no-go class changes.

fn-runTelegramVariantVoiceWave.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram voice-call attempt on the chosen app variant.

M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-VIDEO-WAVE.NAME: TelegramVariantVideoWave
M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-VIDEO-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded Telegram video-call attempt on the chosen app variant without inheriting the voice result.

fn-runTelegramVariantVideoWave.PURPOSE: Run one bounded Telegram video-call attempt on the chosen app variant.

M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-COMPARISON.NAME: TelegramVariantComparison
M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-COMPARISON.purpose: Compare app-variant media packets against the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 packets and determine whether the Telegram no-go class changed.

fn-compareTelegramVariant.PURPOSE: Compare one chosen app variant against the completed workaround packets.

M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-EVIDENCE.NAME: TelegramVariantEvidence
M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-EVIDENCE.purpose: Assemble the bounded app-variant handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison packets for the deeper workaround wave.

fn-assembleTelegramVariantEvidence.PURPOSE: Assemble the evidence packet for the chosen Telegram app variant.

M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-DECISION.NAME: TelegramVariantDecision
M-TELEGRAM-VARIANT-DECISION.purpose: Issue the bounded decision for the tested Telegram setup after the deeper Telegram-specific variant wave.

fn-issueTelegramVariantDecision.PURPOSE: Issue the bounded decision for the chosen Telegram app variant.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VARIANT-HYPOTHESIS.NAME: TelegramMobileVariantHypothesis
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VARIANT-HYPOTHESIS.purpose: Freeze the new truth-boundary where the Desktop app variant is unavailable and the next justified Telegram-specific branch moves to a mobile build with an explicit calls-proxy toggle.

fn-freezeTelegramMobileVariantHypothesis.PURPOSE: Freeze the mobile-only workaround boundary after the blocked Desktop variant.

M-PHASE31-SUPERSEDE.NAME: Phase31Supersede
M-PHASE31-SUPERSEDE.purpose: Explicitly supersede the blocked Phase-31 Desktop app-variant tail so execution truth stays clean.

fn-supersedePhase31.PURPOSE: Mark the blocked Desktop app-variant tail as superseded by the mobile-specific branch.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-HANDOFF-PROFILE.NAME: TelegramMobileHandoffProfile
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-HANDOFF-PROFILE.purpose: Freeze the exact mobile handoff profile using the same SOCKS5 path plus an explicit `Use proxy for calls` toggle state.

fn-freezeTelegramMobileHandoffProfile.PURPOSE: Freeze the exact mobile proxy handoff and calls-toggle state.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-CAPTURE.NAME: TelegramMobileCapture
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-CAPTURE.purpose: Define the bounded capture packet for the mobile Telegram wave so it stays directly comparable with the completed Desktop packets.

fn-defineTelegramMobileCapture.PURPOSE: Define the bounded mobile capture surface for the next workaround wave.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VOICE-WAVE.NAME: TelegramMobileVoiceWave
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VOICE-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded mobile Telegram voice-call attempt under the chosen calls-proxy toggle state.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VIDEO-WAVE.NAME: TelegramMobileVideoWave
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-VIDEO-WAVE.purpose: Replay one bounded mobile Telegram video-call attempt under the same handoff profile without inheriting the voice result.

M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-COMPARISON.NAME: TelegramMobileComparison
M-TELEGRAM-MOBILE-COMPARISON.purpose: Compare the new mobile voice and video packets against the completed Desktop packets and determine whether the mobile app behavior changes the Telegram no-go class.

M-SERVER-INGRESS-CORRELATION.NAME: ServerIngressCorrelation
M-SERVER-INGRESS-CORRELATION.purpose: Define one bounded workstation plus server correlation packet that separates handshake-only, eligibility, decode, loop entry, and explicit pre-loop drop on the preserved server-ingress branch.

fn-defineServerIngressCorrelation.PURPOSE: Define the bounded workstation-plus-server evidence packet for server-ingress attribution.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-VOICE.NAME: TelegramCallsServerIngressVoice
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-VOICE.purpose: Run one bounded Telegram Desktop voice packet that attributes the first server-ingress blocker above the preserved governed handoff floor without reopening video.

fn-runTelegramCallsServerIngressVoice.PURPOSE: Run one bounded server-ingress voice packet on the preserved Desktop topology.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-COMPARIS.NAME: TelegramCallsServerIngressComparison
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-COMPARIS.purpose: Compare the new bounded server-ingress voice packet against the preserved Phase-45, Phase-46, Phase-47, and Phase-48 packet set.

fn-compareTelegramCallsServerIngress.PURPOSE: Compare the server-ingress packet against the preserved boundaries and earlier blockers.

M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-DECISION.NAME: TelegramCallsServerIngressDecision
M-TELEGRAM-CALLS-SERVER-INGRESS-DECISION.purpose: Issue one bounded final decision for the server-ingress branch above the preserved Phase-45, Phase-46, Phase-47, and Phase-48 packet set without overstating calls support.

fn-issueTelegramCallsServerIngressDecisi.PURPOSE: Issue one bounded final decision for the server-ingress branch on the preserved topology.
